item.title:
US WAR WITH IRAN COSTS $11.3B IN FIRST WEEK
United States officials say the first six days of the conflict with Iran cost $11.3 billion, nearly $1.9 billion per day, not including ongoing ship operations and personnel costs.
The campaign has deployed two aircraft carriers, struck 5,000+ targets, and fired hundreds of interceptors against drones and missiles, with seven U.S. servicemembers killed.
Defense officials expect up to $50 billion more may be requested to replenish munitions, as lawmakers push for full accounting of the war’s financial and human toll.
item.description:
US WAR WITH IRAN COSTS $11.3B IN FIRST WEEK

United States officials say the first six days of the conflict with Iran cost $11.3 billion, nearly $1.9 billion per day, not including ongoing ship operations and personnel costs.

The campaign has deployed two aircraft carriers, struck 5,000+ targets, and fired hundreds of interceptors against drones and missiles, with seven U.S. servicemembers killed.

Defense officials expect up to $50 billion more may be requested to replenish munitions, as lawmakers push for full accounting of the war’s financial and human toll.

item.title:
ISRAEL CONSIDERS MAJOR GROUND OPERATION IN LEBANON
Israel signals a potential large-scale ground offensive against Hezbollah after the group launched 200+ rockets and drones in recent attacks.
Defense Minister Israel Katz says the military will seize territory directly, sending additional brigades north.
Strikes across southern Lebanon, especially Beirut’s Dahieh district, have killed ~700, while hundreds of thousands flee, as the conflict escalates with no clear resolution in sight.
item.description:
ISRAEL CONSIDERS MAJOR GROUND OPERATION IN LEBANON

Israel signals a potential large-scale ground offensive against Hezbollah after the group launched 200+ rockets and drones in recent attacks.

Defense Minister Israel Katz says the military will seize territory directly, sending additional brigades north.

Strikes across southern Lebanon, especially Beirut’s Dahieh district, have killed ~700, while hundreds of thousands flee, as the conflict escalates with no clear resolution in sight.

item.title:
CITI RAISES OIL FORECASTS AMID IRAN CONFLICT
Citi Research lifts Brent forecasts to $75 (Q1), $78 (Q2), $68 (Q3), up from $73, $70, and $62. Near-term prices could range $80–$100 per barrel.
Analyst Eric Lee says de-escalation is expected once U.S./Israel objectives are met, but attacks on regional energy infrastructure could still trigger 1970s-style oil shocks.
item.description:
CITI RAISES OIL FORECASTS AMID IRAN CONFLICT

Citi Research lifts Brent forecasts to $75 (Q1), $78 (Q2), $68 (Q3), up from $73, $70, and $62. Near-term prices could range $80–$100 per barrel.

Analyst Eric Lee says de-escalation is expected once U.S./Israel objectives are met, but attacks on regional energy infrastructure could still trigger 1970s-style oil shocks.

item.description: IRAN SUPREME LEADER SAYS VENGEANCE IS PRIORITY 'UNTIL FULLY ACHIEVED'

item.title:
RECESSION RISK RISES AS OIL SURGES
Kalshi traders now see a 31% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, up ~11 points amid oil volatility.
Goldman Sachs flags oil as the top risk from the Iran conflict: each sustained 10% price rise adds ~0.2pp to inflation and cuts GDP growth 0.1pp.
Goldman’s forecast: Brent ~$98 in March–April, PCE inflation 2.9% by Dec 2026, GDP growth 2.2%, unemployment peak 4.6%, 12-month recession risk 25%.
The Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts to September and December as inflation stays elevated. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
RECESSION RISK RISES AS OIL SURGES

Kalshi traders now see a 31% chance of a U.S. recession in 2026, up ~11 points amid oil volatility.

Goldman Sachs flags oil as the top risk from the Iran conflict: each sustained 10% price rise adds ~0.2pp to inflation and cuts GDP growth 0.1pp.

Goldman’s forecast: Brent ~$98 in March–April, PCE inflation 2.9% by Dec 2026, GDP growth 2.2%, unemployment peak 4.6%, 12-month recession risk 25%.

The Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts to September and December as inflation stays elevated.
kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnb…

item.title:
HALF OF GLOBAL LNG TANKERS STRANDED IN THE GULF
About 20 LNG carriers — nearly half the global charter fleet — are stuck in the Persian Gulf, as conflict restricts passage through the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship brokers.
Around 20% of global LNG exports come from Gulf producers, including facilities run by QatarEnergy, some of which have halted output after attacks.
Daily LNG freight rates have jumped above $200,000 from under $98,000, while traders expect further price gains after this week’s 40% surge in Asia and Europe.
item.description:
HALF OF GLOBAL LNG TANKERS STRANDED IN THE GULF

About 20 LNG carriers — nearly half the global charter fleet — are stuck in the Persian Gulf, as conflict restricts passage through the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship brokers.

Around 20% of global LNG exports come from Gulf producers, including facilities run by QatarEnergy, some of which have halted output after attacks.

Daily LNG freight rates have jumped above $200,000 from under $98,000, while traders expect further price gains after this week’s 40% surge in Asia and Europe.

item.title:
NO SIGNS OF IRAN REGIME COLLAPSE — U.S. INTEL
U.S. intelligence sees no immediate risk of regime collapse in Iran, nearly two weeks into the U.S.–Israel war, according to sources familiar with the assessments.
While strikes have killed senior officials and weakened Iran’s missile capabilities, analysts say the government’s hold on power remains intact.
Officials in the Trump administration say the war’s main objective is to degrade Iran’s missile, nuclear and naval programs, not force regime change — though Donald Trump has also urged Iranians to overthrow their government and demanded “unconditional surrender.”
item.description:
NO SIGNS OF IRAN REGIME COLLAPSE — U.S. INTEL

U.S. intelligence sees no immediate risk of regime collapse in Iran, nearly two weeks into the U.S.–Israel war, according to sources familiar with the assessments.

While strikes have killed senior officials and weakened Iran’s missile capabilities, analysts say the government’s hold on power remains intact.

Officials in the Trump administration say the war’s main objective is to degrade Iran’s missile, nuclear and naval programs, not force regime change — though Donald Trump has also urged Iranians to overthrow their government and demanded “unconditional surrender.”

item.title: My phone contents were shared with the police colleague I accused of rape
item.description: Police Scotland has been fined £66,000 by a watchdog after it failed to protect sensitive personal information.

item.title:
HORMUZ OIL SHOCK LIKELY SHORT-LIVED: BLACKROCK
Jean Boivin of the BlackRock Investment Institute says the oil supply shock from the Iran conflict should be temporary, lasting weeks rather than months.
Tanker flows through the Strait of Hormuz are running at about 16% of normal levels, which Boivin called “a massive shock for now.”
However, he expects higher oil prices and market adjustments to prevent a prolonged disruption.
item.description:
HORMUZ OIL SHOCK LIKELY SHORT-LIVED: BLACKROCK

Jean Boivin of the BlackRock Investment Institute says the oil supply shock from the Iran conflict should be temporary, lasting weeks rather than months.

Tanker flows through the Strait of Hormuz are running at about 16% of normal levels, which Boivin called “a massive shock for now.”

However, he expects higher oil prices and market adjustments to prevent a prolonged disruption.

item.title:
U.S. MAY START HORMUZ TANKER ESCORTS BY MONTH-END
Chris Wright says the United States Navy could begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz by the end of the month, though forces are currently focused on degrading Iran’s military capabilities.
About 20% of global oil and gas normally passes through the strait, but shipping has largely stalled since the U.S.–Israel operation against Iran began on Feb. 28.
The White House also clarified that no U.S. tanker escorts have taken place yet, after Wright earlier suggested otherwise in a deleted post.
item.description:
U.S. MAY START HORMUZ TANKER ESCORTS BY MONTH-END

Chris Wright says the United States Navy could begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz by the end of the month, though forces are currently focused on degrading Iran’s military capabilities.

About 20% of global oil and gas normally passes through the strait, but shipping has largely stalled since the U.S.–Israel operation against Iran began on Feb. 28.

The White House also clarified that no U.S. tanker escorts have taken place yet, after Wright earlier suggested otherwise in a deleted post.

item.title: AIR FRANCE-KLM TELLS AFP RAISING LONG-HAUL TICKET PRICES DUE TO OIL SHOCK
item.description: AIR FRANCE-KLM TELLS AFP RAISING LONG-HAUL TICKET PRICES DUE TO OIL SHOCK

item.title:
U.S. NAVY NOT READY TO ESCORT HORMUZ TANKERS — ENERGY SECRETARY
Chris Wright says the United States Navy is not yet ready to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, as military assets remain focused on degrading Iran’s offensive capabilities.
He said escorts could begin later this month.
Shipping through the strait has largely halted, helping push oil higher, with West Texas Intermediate crude near $93 and Brent crude around $98.
item.description:
U.S. NAVY NOT READY TO ESCORT HORMUZ TANKERS — ENERGY SECRETARY

Chris Wright says the United States Navy is not yet ready to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, as military assets remain focused on degrading Iran’s offensive capabilities.

He said escorts could begin later this month.

Shipping through the strait has largely halted, helping push oil higher, with West Texas Intermediate crude near $93 and Brent crude around $98.

item.title:
OIL SHOCK LIFTS U.S. INFLATION OUTLOOK — GOLDMAN
Goldman Sachs raised its U.S. inflation forecast and cut its 2026 growth outlook, warning that higher oil prices linked to the Iran conflict are the main economic risk.
The bank expects Brent crude to average $98 in March–April, potentially $110 if flows through the Strait of Hormuz are disrupted for a month, before easing to $71 by Q4 2026.
Goldman now sees headline Personal Consumption Expenditures price index at 2.9% and core PCE at 2.4% by end-2026, while GDP growth is cut to 2.2%.
With inflation stickier, the bank expects the Federal Reserve to delay its first rate cut to September.
item.description:
OIL SHOCK LIFTS U.S. INFLATION OUTLOOK — GOLDMAN

Goldman Sachs raised its U.S. inflation forecast and cut its 2026 growth outlook, warning that higher oil prices linked to the Iran conflict are the main economic risk.

The bank expects Brent crude to average $98 in March–April, potentially $110 if flows through the Strait of Hormuz are disrupted for a month, before easing to $71 by Q4 2026.

Goldman now sees headline Personal Consumption Expenditures price index at 2.9% and core PCE at 2.4% by end-2026, while GDP growth is cut to 2.2%.

With inflation stickier, the bank expects the Federal Reserve to delay its first rate cut to September.
